title: Algorithms and Data Structures Review Quiz

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Hash table" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A structure that maps keys to buckets for fast lookup
wrongAnswers: A search method that repeatedly narrows a sorted range
A method that tries choices and abandons paths that cannot succeed
A sequence of values stored so each item can be accessed by position

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Binary tree" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A tree where each node has at most two children
wrongAnswers: A sorting method that sorts smaller parts and combines them
Storing function results so repeated calls can be answered quickly
A chain of nodes where each node points to another node

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Heap" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A tree-based structure that keeps the highest or lowest priority item easy to remove
wrongAnswers: A sorting method that partitions data around a pivot
A way to describe how resource use grows with input size
A collection where the most recently added item is removed first

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Graph" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A set of vertices connected by edges
wrongAnswers: A method that reuses solutions to overlapping subproblems
A sort that keeps equal items in their original relative order
A collection where the earliest added item is removed first

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Depth-first search" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A graph search that explores one path deeply before backtracking
wrongAnswers: A method that chooses the best immediate option at each step
An ordering of dependent tasks so prerequisites come first
A structure that maps keys to buckets for fast lookup

listOfQuestions.question: Choose the best match for "Breadth-first search" in Algorithms and Data Structures.
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A graph search that explores nearby vertices before moving farther away
wrongAnswers: A method that tries choices and abandons paths that cannot succeed
A sequence of values stored so each item can be accessed by position
A tree where each node has at most two children
